evals.prompt: Set up the Astro project configuration from scratch including astro.config.mjs, tailwind.config.ts, and tsconfig.json for a static site deployed on Vercel with React components.
evals.expected_output: Complete config files with output: static, trailingSlash: always, React integration, Tailwind integration, sitemap, path aliases, and brand color tokens

assertions.text: astro.config.mjs has output: 'static'
astro.config.mjs has trailingSlash: 'always'
astro.config.mjs includes @astrojs/react integration
astro.config.mjs includes @astrojs/tailwind integration
astro.config.mjs includes @astrojs/sitemap integration
Redirects are NOT in astro.config.mjs (handled by vercel.json)
tailwind.config.ts includes brand color tokens (accent-blue #12477d, brand-red #ea4e59)
Components directory is at project root level, not inside src/
Font loading uses preload strategy (not render-blocking)
No banned AI words used (delve, tapestry, realm, leverage, etc.)

evals.prompt: The build is failing with 'Cannot resolve @/utils/schemaData'. Debug and fix the path alias configuration in the Astro project.
evals.expected_output: Explanation that @ alias must be configured in both tsconfig.json and vite config, with the correct mappings for this project structure

assertions.text: Identifies that path aliases need to be configured in tsconfig.json
Shows the correct path alias mapping for this project
Mentions that the components directory is at root level (not src/)
References the actual project structure with March-Impulse-Web- prefix
Provides a concrete fix (not just a description of the problem)
Mentions that Astro uses Vite under the hood for module resolution
No placeholder text remains (TODO, REPLACE, TBD)
No banned AI words used
Output addresses the specific error message from the prompt
Mentions both tsconfig.json paths and vite resolve alias as relevant config locations
